dialogue.t: —Tranquilo, tienes experiencia. Y tu currículum es bueno.
dialogue.en: Calm down, you have experience. And your résumé is good.
dialogue.lit: Calm, you-have experience. And your résumé is good.

dialogue.t: —Eso espero. ¿Llego a tiempo si voy ahora?
dialogue.en: I hope so. Will I get there on time if I go now?
dialogue.lit: That I-hope. I-arrive at time if I-go now?

dialogue.t: —Sí, si vas ahora, sí. Recuerda: no corras como en el tren.
dialogue.en: Yes, if you go now, yes. Remember: don't run like on the train.
dialogue.lit: Yes, if you-go now, yes. Remember: not run like in the train.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, vale, vale. ¿Cómo me veo?
dialogue.en: Haha, okay, okay. How do I look?
dialogue.lit: Haha, okay, okay. How myself I-see?

dialogue.t: —Muy bien. Profesional. La jefa nueva va a estar contenta.
dialogue.en: Very good. Professional. The new boss is going to be happy.
dialogue.lit: Very good. Professional. The boss new goes to to-be happy.

dialogue.t: —Gracias, Lucía. Necesito esto. Estoy cansado de mi trabajo actual.
dialogue.en: Thanks, Lucía. I need this. I'm tired of my current job.
dialogue.lit: Thanks, Lucía. I-need this. I-am tired of my work current.

dialogue.t: —Lo entiendo. Suerte, Marco. ¡Y respira!
dialogue.en: I understand. Good luck, Marco. And breathe!
dialogue.lit: It I-understand. Luck, Marco. And breathe!

dialogue.t: —Gracias. Te llamo después de la entrevista, te lo prometo.
dialogue.en: Thanks. I'll call you after the interview, I promise.
dialogue.lit: Thanks. You I-call after of the interview, you-it I-promise.

dialogue.t: —¡Lucía! ¡Tengo noticias! ¡Me contrataron!
dialogue.en: Lucía! I have news! They hired me!
dialogue.lit: Lucía! I-have news! Me they-hired!

dialogue.t: —¡¿De verdad?! ¡Felicidades, Marco!
dialogue.en: Really?! Congratulations, Marco!
dialogue.lit: Of-truth?! Congratulations, Marco!

dialogue.t: —Sí, llamaron esta tarde. Empiezo el lunes.
dialogue.en: Yes, they called this afternoon. I start Monday.
dialogue.lit: Yes, they-called this afternoon. I-begin the Monday.

dialogue.t: —¿Y el salario? ¿Es mejor que el de antes?
dialogue.en: And the salary? Is it better than before?
dialogue.lit: And the salary? Is better that the of before?

dialogue.t: —Mucho mejor. No puedo creerlo todavía.
dialogue.en: Much better. I still can't believe it.
dialogue.lit: Much better. Not I-can to-believe-it yet.

dialogue.t: —¡Tenemos que celebrar! ¿Cenamos esta noche?
dialogue.en: We have to celebrate! Shall we have dinner tonight?
dialogue.lit: We-have to to-celebrate! We-dine this night?

dialogue.t: —¡Sí! Invito yo, por primera vez en mi vida.
dialogue.en: Yes! I'm inviting, for the first time in my life.
dialogue.lit: Yes! I-invite I, for first time in my life.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, ¡qué milagro! ¿Le decimos a Sofía y a Diego?
dialogue.en: Haha, what a miracle! Shall we tell Sofía and Diego?
dialogue.lit: Haha, what miracle! To-them we-say to Sofía and to Diego?

dialogue.t: —Claro. Y a Diego que traiga al perro, para celebrar todos.
dialogue.en: Of course. And tell Diego to bring the dog, so we all celebrate.
dialogue.lit: Of-course. And to Diego that bring to-the dog, for to-celebrate all.

dialogue.t: —Perfecto. Voy a llamarlos ahora mismo.
dialogue.en: Perfect. I'm going to call them right now.
dialogue.lit: Perfect. I-go to to-call-them now same.

dialogue.t: —Gracias por todo, Lucía. Sin tu ayuda, no estaría tan tranquilo en la entrevista.
dialogue.en: Thanks for everything, Lucía. Without your help, I wouldn't have been so calm in the interview.
dialogue.lit: Thanks for everything, Lucía. Without your help, not I-would-be so calm in the interview.

dialogue.t: —De nada. ¡Esta noche, celebración! Marco con trabajo nuevo, ¡quién lo iba a decir!
dialogue.en: You're welcome. Tonight, celebration! Marco with a new job, who would've thought!
dialogue.lit: Of nothing. This night, celebration! Marco with work new, who it went to to-say!

dialogue.t: —¿Cómo va la rutina, ahora que volvimos del viaje?
dialogue.en: How's the routine going, now that we're back from the trip?
dialogue.lit: How goes the routine, now that we-returned of-the trip?

dialogue.t: —Bien, pero extraño la playa. Y el atardecer.
dialogue.en: Good, but I miss the beach. And the sunset.
dialogue.lit: Good, but I-miss the beach. And the sunset.

dialogue.t: —Te entiendo. Yo todavía tengo la bandeja de entrada llena.
dialogue.en: I get you. I still have a full inbox.
dialogue.lit: You I-understand. I still I-have the tray of entrance full.

dialogue.t: —¿Y la jefa? ¿Está contenta con tus tareas?
dialogue.en: And the boss? Is she happy with your tasks?
dialogue.lit: And the boss? Is happy with your tasks?

dialogue.t: —Más o menos. Pero terminé el informe antes del viernes.
dialogue.en: More or less. But I finished the report before Friday.
dialogue.lit: More or less. But I-finished the report before of-the Friday.

dialogue.t: —Buena noticia. ¿Y sabes lo de Marco? ¡Lo contrataron!
dialogue.en: Good news. And do you know about Marco? They hired him!
dialogue.lit: Good news. And you-know it of Marco? Him they-hired!

dialogue.t: —¡Sí! Estaba muy nervioso antes de la entrevista, pero salió bien.
dialogue.en: Yes! He was really nervous before the interview, but it went well.
dialogue.lit: Yes! He-was very nervous before of the interview, but it-came-out well.

dialogue.t: —Celebramos juntos. Marco invitó, ¡por primera vez!
dialogue.en: We celebrated together. Marco treated, for the first time!
dialogue.lit: We-celebrated together. Marco invited, for first time!

dialogue.t: —Jaja, increíble. Bueno, hoy estoy descansado y quiero trabajar bien.
dialogue.en: Haha, incredible. Well, today I'm rested and want to work well.
dialogue.lit: Haha, incredible. Well, today I-am rested and I-want to-work well.

dialogue.t: —Yo igual. Aunque todavía pienso en las olas y la arena.
dialogue.en: Same here. Though I still think about the waves and the sand.
dialogue.lit: I equal. Although still I-think in the waves and the sand.

dialogue.t: —Tal vez el próximo verano volvemos a la misma playa.
dialogue.en: Maybe next summer we'll go back to the same beach.
dialogue.lit: Maybe the next summer we-return to the same beach.

dialogue.t: —Ojalá. Por ahora, café, y a trabajar.
dialogue.en: Hopefully. For now, coffee, and to work.
dialogue.lit: Hopefully. For now, coffee, and to to-work.

dialogue.t: —Lucía, vi un anuncio de un piso. Quiero mudarme.
dialogue.en: Lucía, I saw an apartment listing. I want to move.
dialogue.lit: Lucía, I-saw an ad of an apartment. I-want to-move-myself.

dialogue.t: —¿Mudarte? ¿Por qué? Tu piso está bien, ¿no?
dialogue.en: Move? Why? Your apartment's fine, isn't it?
dialogue.lit: To-move-you? For what? Your floor is good, no?

dialogue.t: —Está bien, pero pequeño. El nuevo tiene dos habitaciones.
dialogue.en: It's fine, but small. The new one has two bedrooms.
dialogue.lit: Is good, but small. The new has two rooms.

dialogue.t: —¿Y el alquiler? ¿No es muy caro?
dialogue.en: And the rent? Isn't it really expensive?
dialogue.lit: And the rent? Not is very expensive?

dialogue.t: —Un poco más caro, pero ahora gano más en el trabajo nuevo.
dialogue.en: A bit more expensive, but now I earn more at the new job.
dialogue.lit: A little more expensive, but now I-earn more in the work new.

dialogue.t: —Es verdad, ¡tu salario nuevo! ¿Está cerca de aquí?
dialogue.en: True, your new salary! Is it close to here?
dialogue.lit: Is truth, your salary new! Is near of here?

dialogue.t: —No tan cerca. Pero es muy luminoso, con mucha luz natural.
dialogue.en: Not as close. But it's very bright, with a lot of natural light.
dialogue.lit: Not so near. But is very luminous, with much light natural.

dialogue.t: —Eso me gusta. ¿Vas a verlo pronto?
dialogue.en: I like that. Are you going to see it soon?
dialogue.lit: That myself it-pleases. You-go to to-see-it soon?

dialogue.t: —Sí, mañana. ¿Quieres venir conmigo? Necesito otra opinión.
dialogue.en: Yes, tomorrow. Do you want to come with me? I need another opinion.
dialogue.lit: Yes, tomorrow. You-want to-come with-me? I-need other opinion.

dialogue.t: —Claro. ¿Y qué dice Diego? Va a extrañarte como vecino.
dialogue.en: Of course. And what does Diego say? He's going to miss you as a neighbor.
dialogue.lit: Of-course. And what says Diego? Goes to to-miss-you as neighbor.

dialogue.t: —Todavía no le dije nada. Primero quiero verlo y decidir.
dialogue.en: I haven't told him anything yet. First I want to see it and decide.
dialogue.lit: Still not to-him I-said nothing. First I-want to-see-it and to-decide.

dialogue.t: —Buena idea. Pero si te mudas, espero que sigas viniendo al café.
dialogue.en: Good idea. But if you move, I hope you keep coming to the coffee place.
dialogue.lit: Good idea. But if yourself you-move, I-hope that you-continue coming to-the coffee.

dialogue.t: —Buenas. Soy el casero. ¿Lucía y Marco?
dialogue.en: Hi. I'm the landlord. Lucía and Marco?
dialogue.lit: Good. I-am the landlord. Lucía and Marco?

dialogue.t: —Sí, buenas. Gracias por enseñarnos el piso.
dialogue.en: Yes, hi. Thanks for showing us the apartment.
dialogue.lit: Yes, good. Thanks for to-show-us the apartment.

dialogue.t: —Claro. Aquí tienen la cocina. Es pequeña pero funcional.
dialogue.en: Of course. Here's the kitchen. It's small but functional.
dialogue.lit: Of-course. Here you-have the kitchen. Is small but functional.

dialogue.t: —¡Qué luminoso! Las ventanas son grandes.
dialogue.en: So bright! The windows are big.
dialogue.lit: How luminous! The windows are big.

dialogue.t: —Sí, exacto. Y este es el armario, muy grande también.
dialogue.en: Yes, exactly. And this is the closet, also very big.
dialogue.lit: Yes, exact. And this is the closet, very big too.

dialogue.t: —Marco, mira, hay espacio para todas tus camisas.
dialogue.en: Marco, look, there's space for all your shirts.
dialogue.lit: Marco, look, there-is space for all your shirts.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, perfecto. ¿Y cuánto es el alquiler exactamente?
dialogue.en: Haha, perfect. And how much is the rent exactly?
dialogue.lit: Haha, perfect. And how-much is the rent exactly?

dialogue.t: —Seiscientos euros al mes. Agua y luz incluidas.
dialogue.en: Six hundred euros a month. Water and electricity included.
dialogue.lit: Six-hundred euros to-the month. Water and light included.

dialogue.t: —No está mal. ¿Qué piensas, Lucía?
dialogue.en: Not bad. What do you think, Lucía?
dialogue.lit: Not is bad. What you-think, Lucía?

dialogue.t: —Me encanta. Y las habitaciones son grandes. Yo firmaría hoy mismo.
dialogue.en: I love it. And the rooms are big. I'd sign today.
dialogue.lit: Myself it-enchants. And the rooms are big. I would-sign today same.

dialogue.t: —Vale, casi seguro. ¿Puedo firmar mañana, si todo está bien?
dialogue.en: Okay, almost sure. Can I sign tomorrow, if everything's fine?
dialogue.lit: Okay, almost sure. I-can to-sign tomorrow, if everything is good?

dialogue.t: —Sin problema. Aquí tiene mi número, para cualquier pregunta.
dialogue.en: No problem. Here's my number, for any question.
dialogue.lit: Without problem. Here you-have my number, for any question.

dialogue.t: —¡Diego! Gracias por venir a ayudarme con las cajas.
dialogue.en: Diego! Thanks for coming to help me with the boxes.
dialogue.lit: Diego! Thanks for to-come to to-help-me with the boxes.

dialogue.t: —De nada, Marco. ¿Dónde está el camión?
dialogue.en: You're welcome, Marco. Where's the truck?
dialogue.lit: Of nothing, Marco. Where is the truck?

dialogue.t: —Está abajo. Pero esta caja es muy pesada, ten cuidado.
dialogue.en: It's downstairs. But this box is really heavy, be careful.
dialogue.lit: Is below. But this box is very heavy, have care.

dialogue.t: —¿Qué tiene? ¿Libros?
dialogue.en: What's in it? Books?
dialogue.lit: What has? Books?

dialogue.t: —Sí, libros y... algo frágil. Cuidado con esa, por favor.
dialogue.en: Yes, books and... something fragile. Careful with that one, please.
dialogue.lit: Yes, books and... something fragile. Care with that, for favor.

dialogue.t: —Vale, despacio entonces. ¿Y Sofía? ¿No viene a ayudar?
dialogue.en: Okay, slowly then. And Sofía? Isn't she coming to help?
dialogue.lit: Okay, slowly then. And Sofía? Not comes to to-help?

dialogue.t: —Viene después del gimnasio. Trae su fuerza, dice ella.
dialogue.en: She's coming after the gym. She's bringing her strength, she says.
dialogue.lit: Comes after of-the gym. Brings her strength, says she.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, la necesitamos. Esta caja pesa muchísimo.
dialogue.en: Haha, we need it. This box weighs a ton.
dialogue.lit: Haha, her we-need. This box weighs very-much.

dialogue.t: —Espera, te ayudo. Uno, dos, tres... ¡arriba!
dialogue.en: Wait, I'll help you. One, two, three... up!
dialogue.lit: Wait, you I-help. One, two, three... up!

dialogue.t: —¡Gracias! Sin ti y Sofía, no podría hacer esto solo.
dialogue.en: Thanks! Without you and Sofía, I couldn't do this alone.
dialogue.lit: Thanks! Without you and Sofía, not I-could to-do this alone.

dialogue.t: —Para eso están los amigos, ¿no? Como dijiste tú una vez sobre los vecinos.
dialogue.en: That's what friends are for, right? Like you once said about neighbors.
dialogue.lit: For that are the friends, no? As you-said you one time about the neighbors.

dialogue.t: —Tienes razón. Bueno, una caja menos. ¡Quedan veinte!
dialogue.en: You're right. Well, one box down. Twenty left!
dialogue.lit: You-have reason. Well, a box less. Remain twenty!

lessons.titleTgt: La primera noche en el piso nuevo
lessons.titleEn: The first night in the new apartment

dialogue.t: —Qué raro se siente. El piso está tan vacío todavía.
dialogue.en: It feels so strange. The apartment is still so empty.
dialogue.lit: What strange itself it-feels. The apartment is so empty still.

dialogue.t: —Sí, hay eco cuando hablas. Necesitas muebles.
dialogue.en: Yeah, there's an echo when you talk. You need furniture.
dialogue.lit: Yes, there-is echo when you-speak. You-need furniture.

dialogue.t: —Lo sé. Solo tengo la cama y dos cajas como mesa.
dialogue.en: I know. I only have the bed and two boxes as a table.
dialogue.lit: It I-know. Only I-have the bed and two boxes as table.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, creativo. ¿Quieres que te ayude a comprar muebles este fin de semana?
dialogue.en: Haha, creative. Do you want me to help you buy furniture this weekend?
dialogue.lit: Haha, creative. You-want that you I-help to to-buy furniture this end of week?

dialogue.t: —Me encantaría. Quiero que se sienta acogedor, no vacío.
dialogue.en: I'd love that. I want it to feel cozy, not empty.
dialogue.lit: Myself it-would-enchant. I-want that itself it-feels cozy, not empty.

dialogue.t: —Vamos a instalarte bien. Un sofá, una mesa, y ya está más cómodo.
dialogue.en: We'll get you settled in well. A sofa, a table, and it's already cozier.
dialogue.lit: We-go to to-install-you well. A sofa, a table, and already is more comfortable.

dialogue.t: —Gracias. Es raro vivir solo después de tantos años con compañeros.
dialogue.en: Thanks. It's strange living alone after so many years with roommates.
dialogue.lit: Thanks. Is strange to-live alone after of so-many years with companions.

dialogue.t: —Te vas a acostumbrar. Y siempre puedes llamarme si te sientes solo.
dialogue.en: You'll get used to it. And you can always call me if you feel lonely.
dialogue.lit: Yourself you-go to to-accustom. And always you-can to-call-me if yourself you-feel alone.

dialogue.t: —Gracias, Lucía. Significa mucho. ¿Quieres el primer café aquí, en mi piso nuevo?
dialogue.en: Thanks, Lucía. It means a lot. Want the first coffee here, in my new place?
dialogue.lit: Thanks, Lucía. Means much. You-want the first coffee here, in my apartment new?

dialogue.t: —¡Claro! Aunque sea sentados en cajas.
dialogue.en: Of course! Even if it's sitting on boxes.
dialogue.lit: Of-course! Although it-be seated in boxes.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, exacto. Bienvenida a mi nuevo hogar, todavía vacío pero mío.
dialogue.en: Haha, exactly. Welcome to my new home, still empty but mine.
dialogue.lit: Haha, exact. Welcome to my new home, still empty but mine.

dialogue.t: —Salud, por el piso nuevo. Y por el eco, que pronto desaparecerá.
dialogue.en: Cheers, to the new apartment. And to the echo, which will soon disappear.
dialogue.lit: Health, for the apartment new. And for the echo, that soon will-disappear.

dialogue.t: —¿Cómo va todo, Marco? Trabajo nuevo, piso nuevo... ¡tu vida cambió mucho!
dialogue.en: How's everything going, Marco? New job, new apartment... your life changed a lot!
dialogue.lit: How goes everything, Marco? Work new, apartment new... your life changed much!

dialogue.t: —Sí, jaja. Todo pasó muy rápido este mes.
dialogue.en: Yes, haha. Everything happened very fast this month.
dialogue.lit: Yes, haha. Everything happened very fast this month.

dialogue.t: —¿Y te gusta el trabajo nuevo? ¿La jefa es buena?
dialogue.en: And do you like the new job? Is the boss good?
dialogue.lit: And yourself it-pleases the work new? The boss is good?

dialogue.t: —Sí, muy buena. Y el salario me ayuda mucho con el alquiler.
dialogue.en: Yes, very good. And the salary helps me a lot with the rent.
dialogue.lit: Yes, very good. And the salary me helps much with the rent.

dialogue.t: —¿Ya compraste muebles para el piso?
dialogue.en: Did you already buy furniture for the apartment?
dialogue.lit: Already you-bought furniture for the apartment?

dialogue.t: —Sí, un sofá y una mesa. Ya no hay eco, ya no está vacío.
dialogue.en: Yes, a sofa and a table. There's no more echo, it's not empty anymore.
dialogue.lit: Yes, a sofa and a table. Already not there-is echo, already not is empty.

dialogue.t: —Qué bien. ¿Y Diego? ¿Extraña al vecino viejo?
dialogue.en: Great. And Diego? Does he miss the old neighbor?
dialogue.lit: How good. And Diego? Misses to-the neighbor old?

dialogue.t: —Dice que sí, jaja. Pero todavía vivo cerca, no tan lejos.
dialogue.en: He says yes, haha. But I still live close by, not that far.
dialogue.lit: Says that yes, haha. But still I-live near, not so far.

dialogue.t: —Es verdad. Y Sofía y Diego me ayudaron mucho con las cajas.
dialogue.en: True. And Sofía and Diego helped me a lot with the boxes.
dialogue.lit: Is truth. And Sofía and Diego me helped much with the boxes.

dialogue.t: —Para eso están los amigos. ¿Ya te sientes acogedor en tu casa?
dialogue.en: That's what friends are for. Do you already feel cozy at home?
dialogue.lit: For that are the friends. Already yourself you-feel cozy in your house?

dialogue.t: —Sí, mucho más. Y el café contigo aquí ayuda también.
dialogue.en: Yes, much more. And coffee with you here helps too.
dialogue.lit: Yes, much more. And the coffee with-you here helps too.

dialogue.t: —Siempre. Bueno, vamos a celebrar otra vez: trabajo, piso, y amigos. ¡Salud!
dialogue.en: Always. Well, let's celebrate again: job, apartment, and friends. Cheers!
dialogue.lit: Always. Well, let's-go to to-celebrate other time: work, apartment, and friends. Health!

dialogue.t: —Si tuviera más tiempo libre, viajaría todos los meses.
dialogue.en: If I had more free time, I'd travel every month.
dialogue.lit: If I-had more time free, I-would-travel all the months.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, ¿adónde irías primero?
dialogue.en: Haha, where would you go first?
dialogue.lit: Haha, to-where would-you-go first?

dialogue.t: —Iría a Japón. Siempre quise ver Tokio.
dialogue.en: I'd go to Japan. I've always wanted to see Tokyo.
dialogue.lit: Would-I-go to Japan. Always I-wanted to-see Tokyo.

dialogue.t: —¡Qué bien! Yo aprendería a tocar la guitarra, si tuviera tiempo.
dialogue.en: Nice! I'd learn to play guitar, if I had time.
dialogue.lit: How good! I would-learn to to-play the guitar, if I-had time.

dialogue.t: —¿La guitarra? No sabía que te gustaba la música.
dialogue.en: Guitar? I didn't know you liked music.
dialogue.lit: The guitar? Not I-knew that yourself it-liked the music.

dialogue.t: —Me encanta, pero nunca tengo tiempo. Entre el trabajo y todo...
dialogue.en: I love it, but I never have time. Between work and everything...
dialogue.lit: Myself it-enchants, but never I-have time. Between the work and everything...

dialogue.t: —Te entiendo. Yo cocinaría más, si pudiera.
dialogue.en: I get you. I'd cook more, if I could.
dialogue.lit: You I-understand. I would-cook more, if I-could.

dialogue.t: —¿Tú? Pero si nunca cocinas, Marco.
dialogue.en: You? But you never cook, Marco.
dialogue.lit: You? But if never you-cook, Marco.

dialogue.t: —¡Exacto! Por eso digo 'si pudiera'. Ojalá tuviera más tiempo para aprender.
dialogue.en: Exactly! That's why I say 'if I could.' I wish I had more time to learn.
dialogue.lit: Exact! For that I-say 'if I-could.' Hopefully I-had more time for to-learn.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, vale. Bueno, podríamos empezar con algo fácil este fin de semana.
dialogue.en: Haha, okay. Well, we could start with something easy this weekend.
dialogue.lit: Haha, okay. Well, we-could to-begin with something easy this end of week.

dialogue.t: —¿En serio? ¿Tú me enseñarías a cocinar?
dialogue.en: Really? You'd teach me to cook?
dialogue.lit: In serious? You me would-teach to to-cook?

dialogue.t: —Claro. Sería divertido. ¡Y tal vez aprendes algo nuevo, por una vez!
dialogue.en: Of course. It'd be fun. And maybe you learn something new, for once!
dialogue.lit: Of-course. Would-be fun. And maybe you-learn something new, for one time!

dialogue.t: —Bueno, primera lección: cómo cortar una cebolla sin llorar.
dialogue.en: Okay, first lesson: how to cut an onion without crying.
dialogue.lit: Well, first lesson: how to-cut an onion without to-cry.

dialogue.t: —¿Sin llorar? Eso es imposible, ¿no?
dialogue.en: Without crying? That's impossible, right?
dialogue.lit: Without to-cry? That is impossible, no?

dialogue.t: —Casi imposible. Pero ayuda cortarla cerca del agua.
dialogue.en: Almost impossible. But it helps to cut it near water.
dialogue.lit: Almost impossible. But helps to-cut-it near of-the water.

dialogue.t: —Vale, lo intento. ¿Y después qué hago con la sartén?
dialogue.en: Okay, I'll try it. And then what do I do with the pan?
dialogue.lit: Okay, it I-try. And after what I-do with the pan?

dialogue.t: —Pones aceite, esperas, y después la cebolla. Sigue la receta.
dialogue.en: You put oil, wait, and then the onion. Follow the recipe.
dialogue.lit: You-put oil, you-wait, and after the onion. Follow the recipe.

dialogue.t: —¡Ay, creo que está quemando! ¿Qué hago?
dialogue.en: Oh, I think it's burning! What do I do?
dialogue.lit: Oh, I-believe that is burning! What I-do?

dialogue.t: —¡Marco! Baja el fuego, rápido. No tan caliente.
dialogue.en: Marco! Lower the heat, quick. Not so hot.
dialogue.lit: Marco! Lower the fire, fast. Not so hot.

dialogue.t: —Vale, vale. Ya está mejor. Huele bien, ¿no?
dialogue.en: Okay, okay. It's better now. It smells good, right?
dialogue.lit: Okay, okay. Already is better. Smells good, no?

dialogue.t: —Sí, mucho mejor. ¿Ves? No es tan difícil cocinar.
dialogue.en: Yes, much better. See? Cooking isn't that hard.
dialogue.lit: Yes, much better. You-see? Not is so difficult to-cook.

dialogue.t: —Con tu ayuda, no. Solo, seguro que quemaría todo.
dialogue.en: With your help, no. Alone, I'm sure I'd burn everything.
dialogue.lit: With your help, no. Alone, sure that I-would-burn everything.

dialogue.lit: Save the tears for tomorrow, dame of honor. Today, only joy.

dialogue.t: —¿Estás listo, Diego? Hoy es el día, por fin.
dialogue.en: Are you ready, Diego? Today's the day, finally.
dialogue.lit: You-are ready, Diego? Today is the day, for end.

dialogue.t: —Más listo que nunca, Marco. Aunque las manos no dejan de temblarme.
dialogue.en: More ready than ever, Marco. Though my hands won't stop shaking.
dialogue.lit: More ready that never, Marco. Although the hands not stop to-tremble-me.

dialogue.t: —Es normal, tranqui. Mira, ya viene, ahí está ella.
dialogue.en: It's normal, relax. Look, here she comes, there she is.
dialogue.lit: Is normal, calm. Look, already comes, there is she.

dialogue.t: —Diego... prometo quererte en lo bueno y en lo malo, para toda la vida.
dialogue.en: Diego... I promise to love you in good times and bad, for my whole life.
dialogue.lit: Diego... I-promise to-love-you in the good and in the bad, for all the life.

dialogue.t: —Y yo prometo lo mismo, desde aquella copa de vino torpe hasta hoy y siempre.
dialogue.en: And I promise the same, from that clumsy wine glass until today and forever.
dialogue.lit: And I promise the same, since that glass of wine clumsy until today and always.

dialogue.t: —Con el poder que me da este jardín, los declaro marido y mujer.
dialogue.en: With the power given to me by this garden, I now pronounce you husband and wife.
dialogue.lit: With the power that to-me gives this garden, them I-declare husband and wife.

dialogue.t: —¡Por fin! ¡Sí! ¡Felicidades a los novios!
dialogue.en: Finally! Yes! Congratulations to the newlyweds!
dialogue.lit: For end! Yes! Congratulations to the newlyweds!

dialogue.t: —Lucía, estás llorando otra vez, ¿eh? Y yo también, no te voy a mentir.
dialogue.en: Lucía, you're crying again, huh? And me too, I won't lie to you.
dialogue.lit: Lucía, you-are crying other time, eh? And I too, not you I-go to to-lie.

dialogue.t: —Son lágrimas de alegría, Marco. Mira a esos dos, qué felices se ven.
dialogue.en: These are tears of joy, Marco. Look at those two, how happy they look.
dialogue.lit: Are tears of joy, Marco. Look at those two, how happy themselves they-see.

dialogue.t: —Todo empezó con un torpe que tiró una copa de vino. Y mira ahora.
dialogue.en: It all started with a clumsy guy who knocked over a wine glass. And look now.
dialogue.lit: Everything began with a clumsy that threw a glass of wine. And look now.

dialogue.t: —La vida es así, ¿no? Pequeños momentos que cambian todo.
dialogue.en: Life's like that, isn't it? Small moments that change everything.
dialogue.lit: The life is thus, no? Small moments that change everything.

dialogue.t: —Es verdad. Bueno, ¡a bailar! Y esta vez, sin pisar a nadie, lo prometo.
dialogue.en: True. Well, time to dance! And this time, without stepping on anyone, I promise.
dialogue.lit: Is truth. Well, to to-dance! And this time, without to-step to nobody, it I-promise.

dialogue.t: —¡Mirad a los novios bailando el vals! Qué pasada, de verdad.
dialogue.en: Look at the newlyweds dancing the waltz! How amazing, truly.
dialogue.lit: Look at the newlyweds dancing the waltz! What pass, of truth.

dialogue.t: —Diego nunca me dijo que sabía bailar tan bien.
dialogue.en: Diego never told me he knew how to dance so well.
dialogue.lit: Diego never to-me said that he-knew to-dance so well.

dialogue.t: —Tomó clases en secreto, igual que yo hace un año. Mira cómo gira.
dialogue.en: He took classes in secret, just like I did a year ago. Look how he spins her.
dialogue.lit: He-took classes in secret, equal that I makes a year. Look how he-turns.

dialogue.t: —¡Marco! ¿Vamos nosotros también a la pista de baile?
dialogue.en: Marco! Shall we go to the dance floor too?
dialogue.lit: Marco! We-go we too to the floor of dance?

dialogue.t: —Claro, vamos. Después de tantas clases, ya bailo casi sin esfuerzo.
dialogue.en: Sure, let's go. After so many classes, I dance almost effortlessly now.
dialogue.lit: Of-course, let's-go. After of so-many classes, already I-dance almost without effort.

dialogue.t: —¿Casi? Todavía me pisas el pie de vez en cuando, Marco.
dialogue.en: Almost? You still step on my foot now and then, Marco.
dialogue.lit: Almost? Still me you-step the foot of time in when, Marco.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, vale, vale. Pero mucho menos que al principio, admítelo.
dialogue.en: Haha, okay, okay. But way less than at the start, admit it.
dialogue.lit: Haha, okay, okay. But much less that at the beginning, admit-it.

dialogue.t: —Es verdad, eso sí. Mira, Sofía está enseñándole pasos nuevos a Lucía.
dialogue.en: True, that's fair. Look, Sofía's teaching Lucía some new steps.
dialogue.lit: Is truth, that yes. Look, Sofía is teaching-to-her steps new to Lucía.

dialogue.t: —Qué bonito ver a todos juntos otra vez, en la misma pista de baile.
dialogue.en: How lovely to see everyone together again, on the same dance floor.
dialogue.lit: How pretty to-see to all together other time, in the same floor of dance.

dialogue.t: —Un año entero de cambios, y aquí seguimos, los cuatro, bailando.
dialogue.en: A whole year of changes, and here we still are, the four of us, dancing.
dialogue.lit: A year entire of changes, and here we-continue, the four, dancing.

dialogue.t: —Espero que sigamos así por muchos años más, de verdad.
dialogue.en: I hope we keep going like this for many more years, truly.
dialogue.lit: I-hope that we-continue thus for many years more, of truth.

dialogue.t: —Yo también. Bueno, ¿una vuelta más antes de que se acabe la canción?
dialogue.en: Me too. Well, one more turn before the song ends?
dialogue.lit: I too. Well, a turn more before that itself it-finish the song?

dialogue.t: —Qué boda tan bonita, ¿no? Todavía no me lo creo.
dialogue.en: What a beautiful wedding, huh? I still can't believe it.
dialogue.lit: What wedding so pretty, no? Still not to-me it I-believe.

dialogue.t: —Lo sé. Diego y su esposa, qué raro suena eso todavía, '¡su esposa!'.
dialogue.en: I know. Diego and his wife, that still sounds so strange, 'his wife!'
dialogue.lit: It I-know. Diego and his wife, how strange sounds that still, 'his wife!'

dialogue.t: —Jaja, te vas a acostumbrar. ¿Bailaste mucho ayer? Te vi en la pista toda la noche.
dialogue.en: Haha, you'll get used to it. Did you dance a lot yesterday? I saw you on the dance floor all night.
dialogue.lit: Haha, yourself you-go to to-accustom. You-danced much yesterday? You I-saw in the floor all the night.

dialogue.t: —Muchísimo. Y casi no pisé a nadie, ¿eh? Progreso real.
dialogue.en: So much. And I barely stepped on anyone, huh? Real progress.
dialogue.lit: Very-much. And almost not I-stepped to nobody, eh? Progress real.

dialogue.t: —Jaja, lo noté. Oye, ¿cuánto tiempo te quedas, Lucía, antes de volver?
dialogue.en: Haha, I noticed. Hey, how long are you staying, Lucía, before going back?
dialogue.lit: Haha, it I-noticed. Hey, how-much time yourself you-stay, Lucía, before to-return?

dialogue.t: —Una semana más. Quiero aprovechar cada minuto con vosotros.
dialogue.en: One more week. I want to make the most of every minute with you all.
dialogue.lit: A week more. I-want to-take-advantage each minute with you-all.

dialogue.t: —Perfecto, porque tenemos planes. Marco quiere que veas la cafetería de día, sin la boda como distracción.
dialogue.en: Perfect, because we have plans. Marco wants you to see the coffee shop in daylight, without the wedding as a distraction.
dialogue.lit: Perfect, because we-have plans. Marco wants that you-see the coffeehouse of day, without the wedding as distraction.

dialogue.t: —Me encantaría. Y quiero ver tu medalla otra vez, Sofía, en persona esta vez.
dialogue.en: I'd love that. And I want to see your medal again, Sofía, in person this time.
dialogue.lit: Myself it-would-enchant. And I-want to-see your medal other time, Sofía, in person this time.

dialogue.t: —Claro, la tengo guardada con cuidado. Bueno, ¿plan para hoy entonces?
dialogue.en: Of course, I keep it carefully stored. Well, what's the plan for today then?
dialogue.lit: Of-course, it I-have kept with care. Well, plan for today then?

dialogue.t: —Café en la cafetería de Marco, claro. ¿Dónde más?
dialogue.en: Coffee at Marco's coffee shop, of course. Where else?
dialogue.lit: Coffee in the coffeehouse of Marco, of-course. Where more?

dialogue.t: —Jaja, siempre el café. Algunas cosas, de verdad, nunca cambian.
dialogue.en: Haha, always the coffee. Some things, truly, never change.
dialogue.lit: Haha, always the coffee. Some things, of truth, never change.

dialogue.t: —Y qué bueno que así sea. Vamos, el primer café del día nos espera.
dialogue.en: And how good that it's that way. Let's go, the first coffee of the day awaits us.
dialogue.lit: And how good that thus it-be. Let's-go, the first coffee of the day us waits.

dialogue.t: —Bueno, este es el andén. Mi tren sale en diez minutos.
dialogue.en: Well, this is the platform. My train leaves in ten minutes.
dialogue.lit: Well, this is the platform. My train leaves in ten minutes.

dialogue.t: —No quiero que te vayas otra vez, aunque sé que tienes que hacerlo.
dialogue.en: I don't want you to leave again, even though I know you have to.
dialogue.lit: Not I-want that yourself you-go other time, although I-know that you-have to-do-it.

dialogue.t: —Lo sé, Sofía. Pero ahora es diferente, ¿no? Ya sé el camino de vuelta.
dialogue.en: I know, Sofía. But it's different now, isn't it? I already know the way back.
dialogue.lit: It I-know, Sofía. But now is different, no? Already I-know the road of return.

dialogue.t: —Es verdad. Y vamos a visitarte el mes que viene, te lo prometo.
dialogue.en: True. And we're going to visit you next month, I promise.
dialogue.lit: Is truth. And we-go to to-visit-you the month that comes, you it I-promise.

dialogue.t: —Más os vale. Echo de menos las tardes de café aquí, cada día.
dialogue.en: You'd better. I miss the coffee afternoons here, every day.
dialogue.lit: More you-all is-worth. I-miss less the afternoons of coffee here, each day.

dialogue.t: —Y nosotros a ti. Pero mira todo lo que pasó este año: la boda, la cafetería...
dialogue.en: And we miss you too. But look at everything that happened this year: the wedding, the coffee shop...
dialogue.lit: And we to you. But look everything it that happened this year: the wedding, the coffeehouse...

dialogue.t: —Tu medalla, tu ascenso, mi negocio, el amor de Diego. Qué año, de verdad.
dialogue.en: Your medal, your promotion, my business, Diego's love. What a year, truly.
dialogue.lit: Your medal, your ascent, my business, the love of Diego. What year, of truth.

dialogue.t: —Última llamada para el tren a las quince diez. Última llamada.
dialogue.en: Final call for the 3:10 train. Final call.
dialogue.lit: Last call for the train at the fifteen ten. Last call.

dialogue.t: —Ese es el mío. Vale, abrazo grupal, rápido, antes de que llore otra vez.
dialogue.en: That's mine. Okay, group hug, quick, before I cry again.
dialogue.lit: That is the mine. Okay, hug group, fast, before that I-cry other time.

dialogue.t: —Esto no es un adiós, Lucía. Nunca lo fue, desde el principio.
dialogue.en: This isn't a goodbye, Lucía. It never was, from the beginning.
dialogue.lit: This not is a goodbye, Lucía. Never it was, since the beginning.

dialogue.t: —Tienes razón, Marco. Es un hasta pronto, como siempre. Os quiero, a los tres.
dialogue.en: You're right, Marco. It's a see-you-soon, like always. I love you, all three of you.
dialogue.lit: You-have reason, Marco. Is an until soon, as always. You-all I-love, to the three.

dialogue.t: —Y nosotros a ti. Llámanos al llegar, ¿eh? Y vuelve pronto.
dialogue.en: And we love you too. Call us when you arrive, okay? And come back soon.
dialogue.lit: And we to you. Call-us at to-arrive, eh? And return soon.

dialogue.t: —¿Sabéis qué día es hoy? Hace exactamente un año que nos conocimos.
dialogue.en: Do you know what day it is today? It's been exactly a year since we met.
dialogue.lit: You-all-know what day is today? Makes exactly a year that us we-met.

dialogue.t: —¡Un año! Marco, te pregunté si eras de aquí, en esta misma mesa.
dialogue.en: A year! Marco, I asked you if you were from here, at this very table.
dialogue.lit: A year! Marco, you I-asked if you-were from here, in this same table.

dialogue.t: —Y mira todo lo que pasó desde entonces. No me lo puedo creer, de verdad.
dialogue.en: And look at everything that happened since then. I really can't believe it.
dialogue.lit: And look everything it that happened since then. Not to-me it I-can to-believe, of truth.

dialogue.t: —El viaje a la playa, el apagón, la feria, las clases de baile...
dialogue.en: The trip to the beach, the blackout, the fair, the dance classes...
dialogue.lit: The trip to the beach, the blackout, the fair, the classes of dance...

dialogue.t: —Mi ascenso, tu cafetería, mi medalla, la boda de Diego.
dialogue.en: My promotion, your coffee shop, my medal, Diego's wedding.
dialogue.lit: My ascent, your coffeehouse, my medal, the wedding of Diego.

dialogue.t: —Lo logramos, ¿no? Cada uno a su manera, pero juntos al final.
dialogue.en: We did it, didn't we? Each in our own way, but together in the end.
dialogue.lit: It we-achieved, no? Each one to his manner, but together at end.

dialogue.t: —Mirar atrás así, da un poco de vértigo. Tanto cambió, y tan rápido.
dialogue.en: Looking back like this gives me a bit of vertigo. So much changed, and so fast.
dialogue.lit: To-look behind thus, gives a little of vertigo. So-much changed, and so fast.

dialogue.t: —Pero lo más importante no cambió: seguimos aquí, los cuatro, con café.
dialogue.en: But the most important thing didn't change: we're still here, the four of us, with coffee.
dialogue.lit: But the most important not changed: we-continue here, the four, with coffee.

dialogue.t: —Es verdad. Bueno, ¿y qué creéis que viene ahora? ¿Qué nos espera el próximo año?
dialogue.en: True. Well, what do you think is coming now? What awaits us next year?
dialogue.lit: Is truth. Well, and what you-all-believe that comes now? What us waits the next year?

dialogue.t: —Ni idea, la verdad. Pero si fue así de bonito este año, no tengo miedo de lo que viene.
dialogue.en: No idea, honestly. But if this year was this beautiful, I'm not afraid of what's coming.
dialogue.lit: Not idea, the truth. But if was thus of pretty this year, not I-have fear of it that comes.

dialogue.t: —Para siempre amigos, pase lo que pase. Eso sí lo sé seguro.
dialogue.en: Friends forever, whatever happens. That much I know for sure.
dialogue.lit: For always friends, pass it that pass. That yes it I-know sure.

dialogue.t: —Para siempre amigos. Bueno... ¿brindamos? Por el primer hola, y por todos los que vienen.
dialogue.en: Friends forever. Well... shall we toast? To the first hello, and to all the ones still to come.
dialogue.lit: For always friends. Well... we-toast? For the first hello, and for all the that come.
